subject: [PATCH 2/4] mm: bypass mmap_miss heuristic for VM_EXEC readahead

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara noted that moving the VM_EXEC readahead logic earlier would disable VM_HUGEPAGE handling for VM_EXEC vmas, and instead suggested disabling the mmap_miss logic for VM_EXEC vmas by adding a conditional check in the if statement.
reviews.raw_body:
I can see the problem but I'm not sure what you propose is the right fix.
If you move the VM_EXEC logic earlier, you'll effectively disable
VM_HUGEPAGE handling for VM_EXEC vmas which I don't think we want. So
shouldn't we rather disable mmap_miss logic for VM_EXEC vmas like:

	if (!(vm_flags & (VM_SEQ_READ | VM_EXEC))) {
		...
	}

								Honza

2026-03-10.patch_summary: This patch modifies the behavior of the mmap_miss heuristic in the Linux kernel's memory management (mm) module. The mmap_miss counter tracks whether readahead is useful for mmap'd file access and disables all readahead, including targeted VM_EXEC readahead, when it exceeds a certain threshold. However, on arm64 with 64K base pages, the decrement paths are inactive due to specific conditions, causing the counter to monotonically increase past the threshold after 100 page faults. The patch fixes this issue by moving the VM_EXEC readahead block above the mmap_miss check, allowing targeted readahead for executable memory without being gated by the heuristic.
